text.title: Rotate First, Revert Second
text.summary: When a verified secret lands, rotation precedes any git history rewrite — reverting first leaves the live key reachable in clones.

section.title: Order of operations
section.p: Once a credential is on a public branch or any shared remote, the only remediation that matters is rotating the credential at the issuer. History rewrites and force-pushes do not invalidate a token — the token must be revoked.

rule.statement: The first action on a verified secret finding MUST be rotation at the issuer (AWS IAM key delete, GitHub token revoke, OpenAI/Anthropic key revoke, Stripe key roll). Only after the issuer confirms revocation does the responder rewrite history if desired. The rotation event MUST be logged in the incident channel with the issuer's confirmation ID and the new secret stored in the team secret manager (Vault, 1Password, AWS Secrets Manager). The PR that introduced the leak MUST be closed without merge until the new secret is wired in.
rule.rationale: An attacker who clones the repo before a force-push still has the leaked token; a `git filter-repo` on the remote does not delete the value from anyone's local copy or any cache, fork, or backup. Issuer-side revocation is the only operation that breaks the attacker's access.

example.code: # AWS aws iam delete-access-key --access-key-id AKIA... --user-name ci-bot # GitHub gh api -X DELETE /user/tokens/$TOKEN_ID # OpenAI / Anthropic — UI-only, capture confirmation ID in incident channel
example.note: Each call returns confirmation; the responder pastes the IDs into the incident record.

example.code: git push --force origin main # delete the commit and call it done
example.note: The token is still live in caches, forks, and CI logs; no revocation has happened.

antipattern.description: Rotating the secret silently, without logging the issuer's confirmation ID, "to avoid embarrassment."
antipattern.reason: Future audits cannot prove revocation; SOC2 and ISO 27001 control evidence requires the confirmation artifact.

section.reference: GitHub guidance on revoked tokens still appearing in history.
detect-secrets — baseline-driven rollout for legacy repos.
